title: Polymarket Analytics: Market Data Guide
description: Polymarket analytics explained through volume, liquidity, top holders, market movement, data dashboards, and ScreenOdds signal checks.

sections.heading: Why Polymarket analytics is a high-value page
paragraphs: DataForSEO shows `polymarket analytics` at 1,000 US monthly searches with keyword difficulty of 1 and a CPC above $47. That is the exact type of query ScreenOdds should target: commercial intent, low organic difficulty, and a reader who wants data rather than a generic prediction-market definition.

This page should sit above narrower pages such as Polymarket tracker, Polymarket volume, and prediction-market arbitrage. Analytics is the broad workflow; those pages explain specific checks inside that workflow.

sections.heading: What good analytics should show
paragraphs: A useful Polymarket analytics view should show more than the current yes price. The minimum useful stack is recent price movement, volume, liquidity, open interest, top holder concentration, and market age.

The reason is simple: a market can move sharply on low volume, and that move can look important on a chart. ScreenOdds treats volume and liquidity as the first context layer before interpreting any headline price change.

sections.heading: How to read top-holder and trader data
paragraphs: Top-holder data can help readers spot concentration risk. If one account owns a large share of the yes side, the market may be less representative than the headline percentage suggests.

Trader data is useful, but it should not be treated as a magic ranking system. A strong historical record in one category does not automatically transfer to politics, entertainment, crypto, or macro markets.

sections.heading: Where analytics pages can mislead readers
paragraphs: The most common mistake is reading every price change as information. Some moves are information, some are liquidity, and some are simply a small order moving a thin market.

Another mistake is ignoring resolution rules. Analytics can show market behavior, but the final outcome still depends on how the market resolves. A data dashboard cannot fix vague wording or an ambiguous source of truth.

sections.heading: How ScreenOdds will use this cluster
paragraphs: ScreenOdds should use this page as the central guide for analytics intent, then link downward to tracker, volume, and arbitrage pages. That gives Google a clear cluster instead of four pages competing for the same query.

Entertainment pages can link here when a market move needs explanation. If an Oscars, Netflix, or Love Island market jumps, this guide explains how to evaluate whether the move has enough supporting data.

sections.heading: Best use case for readers
paragraphs: Use Polymarket analytics to verify whether a market move has substance. Check volume, liquidity, holder concentration, market age, and resolution rules before deciding whether the chart tells a real story.
ScreenOdds presents this as market literacy, not trading advice. The goal is to understand why a price moved and whether the move deserves attention.

faqs.question: What is Polymarket analytics?
faqs.answer: It is the process of reading market data such as price movement, volume, liquidity, holders, open interest, and market history around Polymarket events.

faqs.question: Is Polymarket analytics the same as a tracker?
faqs.answer: No. Analytics is the broader interpretation layer, while a tracker is a watchlist or monitoring workflow for specific markets.

faqs.question: What is the first metric to check?
faqs.answer: Volume and liquidity should be checked before price movement because thin markets can move sharply without much real information.
